✓ 27 passed✗ 33 failed
1 🗄️ Insecure Data Storage weight 130 / 1000 0% risk · 0/130 pts
Hardcoded secrets, unprotected local storage, sensitive logging.
✓ HIGH Hardcoded Secrets
0
✓ HIGH UserDefaults Only for Non-Sensitive Data
0
✗ HIGH Unencrypted Database (Realm / CoreData / SQLite)
1
A Realm database opened without `encryptionKey`, a Core Data store added without `NSPersistentStoreFileProtectionKey`, or a SQLite database opened without SQLCipher's `sqlite3_key` stores all records in plaintext on disk. On a jailbroken device or via direct iTunes backup extraction the entire database is readable. Always supply an encryption key derived from the Keychain — never hardcode it. The Realm check is project-wide: if `encryptionKey` appears anywhere in the codebase, all Realm call sites are considered safe.
Postbox/Sources/Database.swift:49let res = sqlite3_open_v2(location, &self.handle, flags, nil)Peter
✓ HIGH Hardcoded Secrets in Info.plist / xcconfig
0
✓ HIGH Service-Specific Secret Prefixes (AWS / GitHub / Stripe / Slack / Google)
0
✓ HIGH Credentials in Committed .env File
0
✓ MEDIUM Sensitive Responses Persisted by URLCache / WKWebView
0
✗ LOW Sensitive Data in Print / Debug Logs
1
Logging passwords, tokens, or secrets via `print` or `debugPrint` writes sensitive data to device logs readable by other processes or extractable from crash reports. Redact sensitive fields or use `os_log` / `Logger` with the `.private` privacy flag.
MathRender/Tokenization/MTAtomTokenizer.swift:272print("\n=== Tokenizing multi-char text: '\(text)' ===")Ilya Laktyushin
2 💥 Crash Factors weight 120 / 1000 64% risk · 77/120 pts
Force unwraps, try!, as!, IUOs, deadlocks, runtime selector failures.
✗ HIGH Never Force Try (try!)
75
`try!` crashes the app with a fatal error if the throwing expression throws. Use `try?` to get an optional result or `do { try … } catch { … }` to handle the error explicitly.

✗ HIGH Avoid DispatchQueue.main.sync
2
Calling `DispatchQueue.main.sync` from the main thread causes a guaranteed deadlock that freezes the app - an availability failure and a denial-of-service surface. Use `DispatchQueue.main.async` instead, or restructure to avoid dispatching to main from main.
NotificationService/Sources/NotificationService.swift:2965DispatchQueue.main.sync(execute: work)Isaac
Sources/Helpers/DisplayLinkService.swift:42DispatchQueue.main.sync {overtake
✗ MEDIUM Avoid Implicitly Unwrapped Optionals (IUOs)
39
Implicitly unwrapped optionals (`Type!`) bypass optional safety: if nil when accessed, the app crashes. Use them only for IBOutlets and properties guaranteed to be set before first access (e.g., in `viewDidLoad`). For everything else, use a regular optional `?` and unwrap explicitly.

✗ MEDIUM fatalError / preconditionFailure in Production Code
1849 (showing 100)
`fatalError` and `preconditionFailure` terminate the process immediately with no recovery path. They are also common placeholders left over from Xcode-generated stubs. Replace with a recoverable error, a graceful fallback, or ensure the path is truly unreachable before keeping them in production.

✗ MEDIUM Catastrophic ReDoS Regex Pattern
2
A regular expression passed to `NSRegularExpression` / `Regex` contains nested quantifiers (e.g. `(a+)+`, `(\w+\d+)+`) that cause catastrophic backtracking. An attacker who controls input matched against this pattern can force exponential backtracking and hang the process. Rewrite using possessive quantifiers or an RE2-safe equivalent.
Sources/Data and Storage/WebBrowserDomainController.swift:41let domainRegex = try? NSRegularExpression(pattern: "^(https?://)?([a-zA-Z0-9-]+\\.?)*([a-zA-Z]*)?(:…Ilya Laktyushin
Sources/Data and Storage/WebBrowserDomainController.swift:42let pathRegex = try? NSRegularExpression(pattern: "^(https?://)?([a-zA-Z0-9-]+\\.)+[a-zA-Z]{2,6}/", …Ilya Laktyushin
3 🧨 Unsafe & Deprecated Constructs weight 60 / 1000 0% risk · 0/60 pts
Legacy ObjC APIs, deprecated calls, compile-time literals.
✗ HIGH Dangerous C String Functions
9
`gets`, `sprintf`, `strcpy`, `strcat`, `scanf`, and related functions perform no bounds checking — a too-short buffer causes memory corruption exploitable for code execution. Use length-limited variants (`snprintf`, `strlcpy`, `strlcat`) or Swift-native string handling instead.
Postbox/Sources/MediaBox.swift:1975strncpy(self.pathBuffer, self.path, 1024)Ali
Postbox/Sources/MediaBox.swift:1976strncat(self.pathBuffer, "/", 1024)Ali
Postbox/Sources/MediaBox.swift:1977strncat(self.pathBuffer, &dirp.pointee.d_name.0, 1024)Ali
Postbox/Sources/MediaBox.swift:2021strncpy(pathBuffer, path, 1024)Ali
Postbox/Sources/MediaBox.swift:2022strncat(pathBuffer, "/", 1024)Ali
Postbox/Sources/MediaBox.swift:2023strncat(pathBuffer, &dirp.pointee.d_name.0, 1024)Ali
Postbox/Sources/TimeBasedCleanup.swift:155strncpy(pathBuffer, path, 1024)Ali
Postbox/Sources/TimeBasedCleanup.swift:156strncat(pathBuffer, "/", 1024)Ali
Postbox/Sources/TimeBasedCleanup.swift:157strncat(pathBuffer, &dirp.pointee.d_name.0, 1024)Ali
✓ HIGH Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Constants (DES / 3DES / RC2 / RC4)
0
✗ HIGH UIWebView Usage (Deprecated, No Security Patches Since iOS 12)
1
`UIWebView` has been deprecated since iOS 12 and removed from the App Store review guidelines. It no longer receives security patches, carries known XSS and memory-safety vulnerabilities, and was rejected by App Store review since April 2020. Replace all `UIWebView` instances with `WKWebView`.
Sources/Items/ChatDocumentGalleryItem.swift:126let webView = UIWebView()Peter
✗ MEDIUM Replace Deprecated openURL
4
`UIApplication.shared.openURL(_:)` was deprecated in iOS 10. Use `open(_:options:completionHandler:)` instead - it lets the system handle the transition and provides a completion callback. Deprecated APIs receive no security maintenance.
Telegram/Share/ShareRootController.swift:76let _ = self.openURL(url)Ilya Laktyushin
Telegram/Share/ShareRootController.swift:103@objc func openURL(_ url: URL) -> Bool {Ilya Laktyushin
Telegram/Share/ShareRootController.swift:111return application.perform(#selector(openURL(_:)), with: url) != nilIlya Laktyushin
PassportUI/Sources/SecureIdAuthFormContentNode.swift:83openURL(url)Peter Iakovlev
✗ LOW Deprecated arc4random in Non-Security Code
11
`arc4random()` and `arc4random_uniform()` are deprecated since Swift 4.2. For UI animations, particle effects, and other non-security randomness, prefer `Int.random(in:)` / `Float.random(in:)` from Swift's standard library — they are faster, type-safe, and not deprecated.
InstantPageUI/Sources/InstantPageMediaPlaylist.swift:221selectedIndex = Int(arc4random_uniform(UInt32(self.items.count)))Peter

5 🧠 Memory Corruption & Exploit Factors weight 80 / 1000 12% risk · 10/80 pts
Unsafe pointers, use-after-free, buffer/integer overflows.
✗ HIGH Unsafe Pointer Usage
566 (showing 100)
Direct use of `UnsafePointer`, `UnsafeMutablePointer`, `UnsafeRawPointer`, `withUnsafeBytes`, etc. bypasses Swift's memory-safety guarantees. Incorrect pointer arithmetic, aliasing, or lifetime management causes undefined behaviour exploitable for memory corruption. Prefer higher-level Swift APIs; review every unsafe block thoroughly.

✗ MEDIUM Manual Reference Counting (Unmanaged<T>)
213 (showing 100)
`Unmanaged<T>` with `passRetained`, `takeRetainedValue`, etc. requires manually balanced retains/releases. A mismatch causes either a use-after-free (exploitable) or a leak. Use `Unmanaged` only for required C-API bridging; validate lifetimes carefully.
LottieMetalTest/Sources/CompareToReferenceRendering.swift:58let colorSpace = Unmanaged<CGColorSpace>.passRetained(lhs.cgImage!.colorSpace!)Isaac

6 🔐 Cryptography Issues weight 110 / 1000 0% risk · 0/110 pts
Weak algorithms, insecure randomness, broken modes, key handling.
✗ HIGH Insecure Randomness in Security Context (arc4random)
3
`arc4random()` / `arc4random_uniform()` are cryptographically weak — found in files that perform encryption, signing, padding, or secure-ID generation. These values must be unpredictable: replace with `SecRandomCopyBytes` or `CryptoKit.SystemRandomNumberGenerator`.
TelegramEngine/SecureId/SecureIdPadding.swift:4var paddingCount = Int(47 + arc4random_uniform(255 - 47))Peter Iakovlev
TelegramEngine/Peers/CreateSecretChat.swift:34return account.network.request(Api.functions.messages.requestEncryption(userId: inputUser, randomId:…Ilya Laktyushin
Sources/SecretChats/SecretChatEncryption.swift:320var remainingCount = Int(arc4random_uniform(UInt32(72 + 1 - take)))Peter Iakovlev
✓ HIGH Weak Hash Algorithm (MD5 / SHA-1)
0
✓ HIGH ECB Cipher Mode
0
✗ HIGH Hardcoded Encryption Key
1
A symmetric key or AES key assigned from a string literal (`let key = "abc123..."`) or a hardcoded `[UInt8]` array is embedded in the binary and extractable with `strings`. Key material must be generated at runtime and stored in the Keychain — never written in source code.
third-party/flatc/Package.swift:20checksum: "d65628c225ef26e0386df003fe47d6b3ec8775c586d7dae1a9ef469a0a9906f1"Mikhail Filimonov
✓ HIGH CBC Mode Without Authentication (MAC)
0
✓ HIGH Private Key Committed to Source
0
✓ MEDIUM Hardcoded IV / Nonce
0
✓ MEDIUM Insufficient PBKDF2 Iterations
0
7 🌐 Network Security weight 90 / 1000 0% risk · 0/90 pts
ATS disabled, missing pinning, plaintext communication.
✗ HIGH Plaintext HTTP URLs
2
Hardcoded `http://` URLs transmit all data — tokens, session cookies, API responses — in cleartext. Any network observer can read or modify traffic (MITM). Upgrade all communications to HTTPS and ensure ATS is not disabled.
TelegramUniversalVideoContent/Sources/HLSVideoJSNativeContentNode.swift:1182SharedHLSVideoJSContext.shared.initializeWhenReady(context: self, urlPrefix: "http://server/\(player…Isaac
XCScheme/src/CreateSchemeManagement.swift:26<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN" "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd…
✓ HIGH Certificate Validation Bypass
0
✗ HIGH URLSessionDelegate Challenge Bypass (unconditional useCredential)
1
`completionHandler(.useCredential, ...)` in `urlSession(_:didReceive:completionHandler:)` without a prior `SecTrustEvaluateWithError` call unconditionally accepts any certificate the server presents — including self-signed ones planted by a MITM proxy. Always evaluate trust before granting a credential; cancel with `.cancelAuthenticationChallenge` on failure.
BrowserUI/Sources/BrowserWebContent.swift:1098completionHandler(.useCredential, credential)Ilya Laktyushin
✗ HIGH ATS Disabled in Info.plist (NSAllowsArbitraryLoads)
3
`NSAllowsArbitraryLoads = true` disables App Transport Security, allowing connections to any HTTP server and bypassing TLS requirements enforced since iOS 9. Remove this key or scope it to specific domains that genuinely require exceptions.
Telegram/WatchApp/tgwatch Watch App/Info.plist:27<key>NSAllowsArbitraryLoads</key>isaac
Telegram/Telegram-iOS/InfoBazel.plist:107<key>NSAllowsArbitraryLoads</key>Ali
Telegram/Telegram-iOS/Info.plist:273<key>NSAllowsArbitraryLoads</key>Peter Iakovlev
✗ HIGH WebView JavaScript Injection / Unsafe Configuration
7
Passing interpolated strings into `evaluateJavaScript` lets attacker-controlled content execute arbitrary JS inside the web view's origin — equivalent to stored XSS. `allowFileAccessFromFileURLs` / `allowUniversalAccessFromFileURLs` lets JS escape the sandbox and read the local filesystem. `allowContentJavaScript = true` (iOS 14+) re-enables JS if it was disabled. Setting `isFraudulentWebsiteWarningEnabled = false` disables Safe Browsing, letting phishing pages load silently. Always sanitize data crossing into the JS bridge; disable file-URL access and keep Safe Browsing enabled.

8 🪪 Authentication & Authorization weight 100 / 1000 0% risk · 0/100 pts
Credential misuse, broken cert validation, session handling.
✓ HIGH Over-Accessible Keychain Items (kSecAttrAccessibleAlways)

9 ⌨️ Input/Output Validation weight 80 / 1000 0% risk · 0/80 pts
Injection, path traversal, command injection.
✗ HIGH Unsafe String Interpolation in SQL Queries
16
Building SQL queries with string interpolation (`"SELECT … WHERE name = '\(userInput)'"`) lets a single-quote break the query structure and execute attacker-controlled SQL. Use parameterized queries: prepare the statement with `?` / `:name` placeholders and bind values via `sqlite3_bind_*` so values are never treated as executable SQL.

✗ HIGH Insecure Deserialization (NSKeyedUnarchiver)
6
`NSKeyedUnarchiver.unarchiveObject(with:)` and `unarchiveTopLevelObjectWithData` allow a malicious archive to instantiate arbitrary `NSObject` subclasses — a code-execution surface. Use the secure-coding initializer `NSKeyedUnarchiver(forReadingFrom:)` with `requiresSecureCoding = true` and an explicit `allowedClasses` set. Also flag `NSKeyedArchiver` used with `requiringSecureCoding: false`.

✗ HIGH Shell Command Injection Risk (Process / NSTask)
1
Constructing shell commands with `Process`/`NSTask` and user-supplied arguments is dangerous: an attacker controlling any part of the command string can inject arbitrary shell commands. Pass arguments as individual array elements — never as a shell-expanded string — and validate all inputs against a strict allowlist.
SharedUtils/Shell/ShellCommandRunner.swift:36let process = Process()
✗ MEDIUM Path Traversal Risk (".." in File Paths)
1
Path segments containing `..` can escape intended directory boundaries when constructed from user-controlled input. Validate and canonicalize all paths; reject inputs containing `..` or use Foundation's `standardized`/`resolvingSymlinksInPath` and compare against an expected root prefix.
swift/runfiles/Runfiles.swift:129!path.hasPrefix("../"),
10 🛡️ Binary Protections weight 40 / 1000 0% risk · 0/40 pts
Obfuscation, debugger/jailbreak detection, integrity checks.
✓ HIGH Debug / Diagnostic Framework in Production
0
✗ MEDIUM No Jailbreak Detection in Codebase
1
No jailbreak/root-detection patterns were found. On a jailbroken device, code-signing is bypassed, sandbox boundaries are removed, and runtime hooks can intercept API calls. For sensitive applications implement active jailbreak detection and respond accordingly.
Project-wideNo jailbreak detection patterns found in the codebase.
✓ MEDIUM No Anti-Tampering Protections Found
0
11 🔍 Privacy Violations weight 50 / 1000 0% risk · 0/50 pts
Excessive permissions, consent gaps, identifier misuse.
✗ MEDIUM Clipboard Data Access
5
Reading from `UIPasteboard.general` without a clear user action can silently harvest sensitive data copied from another app — passwords, credit-card numbers, OTPs. iOS 14+ notifies users of clipboard access. Restrict reads to explicit user interactions; never poll the pasteboard in background tasks.

✓ MEDIUM Declared Permission Without API Usage (Info.plist Mismatch)
0
✗ LOW Device Fingerprinting / Advertising ID
2
Using `advertisingIdentifier`, `ASIdentifierManager`, or carrier identifiers without consent violates App Store Guidelines 5.1.2 and GDPR/CCPA. Request ATT permission before accessing the IDFA; prefer `identifierForVendor` for analytics and anonymise persistent identifiers.

13 🔀 Logic & State-based Exploit Factors weight 30 / 1000 0% risk · 0/30 pts
Race conditions, jailbreak bypasses, insecure IPC, state bugs.
✗ MEDIUM Main Thread Blocking (Thread.sleep)
8
Calling `Thread.sleep(forTimeInterval:)` on the main thread blocks all UI updates and user interaction — a reliable denial-of-service for the UI and a signal of incorrect async design. Use `Task.sleep`, `asyncAfter`, or actor-based scheduling instead.
MediaPlayer/Sources/MediaDataReader.swift:270Thread.sleep(forTimeInterval: 1.0 / 60.0)Isaac
Sources/StorageBox/StorageBox.swift:185Thread.sleep(forTimeInterval: 0.1 + 0.5 * Double(i))Ali
Sources/AccountManager/AccountManagerImpl.swift:105Thread.sleep(forTimeInterval: 0.1 + 0.5 * Double(i))Ali

Sources/Network/MultipartFetch.swift:650Thread.sleep(forTimeInterval: 0.1)Ali
Sources/Network/MultipartFetch.swift:653Thread.sleep(forTimeInterval: 5.0)Ali
TelegramAudio/Sources/ManagedAudioSession.swift:828Thread.sleep(forTimeInterval: 2.0)Ali
SourceKitBazelBSP/SharedUtils/Retry.swift:42Thread.sleep(forTimeInterval: delay)
✗ LOW Notification Observer Not Removed
6
A file that registers `NotificationCenter` observers without a corresponding `removeObserver` call retains stale observers indefinitely. With the selector-based API this causes crashes after deallocation; block-based observers create ghost listeners. Always remove observers in `deinit` or use block-based observation with a stored token.
Public/iOS/AnimationViewBase.swift:55NotificationCenter.default.addObserver(Isaac

14 🔩 Low-level Binary Vulnerabilities weight 20 / 1000 21% risk · 4/20 pts
C/ObjC/C++ memory-corruption primitives and ObjC runtime hazards.
✗ HIGH Raw C Memory Operations (malloc / free / memcpy)
1071 (showing 100)
Direct use of `malloc`, `calloc`, `realloc`, `free`, `memcpy`, `memmove`, or `memset` bypasses Swift's memory safety. Incorrect sizes, double-frees, or use-after-free are exploitable for heap corruption. Prefer Swift collections and `Data`; encapsulate all raw memory behind a reviewed abstraction boundary.

✗ HIGH Objective-C Runtime Hazards (objc_msgSend / NSInvocation / performSelector)
4
Direct `objc_msgSend` calls require a manually correct function-pointer cast — a mismatched return type or calling convention silently corrupts registers or the stack. `performSelector:` via `NSSelectorFromString` dispatches by runtime string with no compile-time signature verification. `NSInvocation` invoked with a mismatched method signature overwrites adjacent stack memory. Only applies to `.m` / `.mm` files.
Source/UIKitRuntimeUtils/UIKitUtils.m:123NSInvocation *inv = [NSInvocation invocationWithMethodSignature:signature];Peter
Source/UIKitRuntimeUtils/UIKitUtils.m:137NSInvocation *inv = [NSInvocation invocationWithMethodSignature:signature];Peter
Source/UIKitRuntimeUtils/UIKitUtils.m:152NSInvocation *inv = [NSInvocation invocationWithMethodSignature:signature];Peter
Source/UIKitRuntimeUtils/UIKitUtils.m:166NSInvocation *inv = [NSInvocation invocationWithMethodSignature:signature];Isaac
✗ MEDIUM Unsafe Buffer Pointer / baseAddress! Access
540 (showing 100)
`UnsafeBufferPointer`, `UnsafeMutableBufferPointer`, `UnsafeRawBufferPointer`, and `.baseAddress!` give direct pointer access to contiguous memory. Out-of-bounds indexing causes memory corruption; force-unwrapping `.baseAddress!` crashes on an empty buffer. Validate bounds before any pointer arithmetic.

## 🚨 Security — Index 91 / 1000

**Checks Failed:** 33 / 60

| Check | Violations |
|-------|------------|
| Insecure Randomness in Security Context (arc4random) | 3 |
| Deprecated arc4random in Non-Security Code | 11 |
| Never Force Try (try!) | 75 |
| Never Force Unwrap (!) | 6798 |
| Avoid Force Casts (as!) | 1056 |
| Avoid DispatchQueue.main.sync | 2 |
| Avoid Implicitly Unwrapped Optionals (IUOs) | 39 |
| Replace Deprecated openURL | 4 |
| Sensitive Data in Print / Debug Logs | 1 |
| Unencrypted Database (Realm / CoreData / SQLite) | 1 |
| fatalError / preconditionFailure in Production Code | 1849 |
| Dangerous C String Functions | 9 |
| UIWebView Usage (Deprecated, No Security Patches Since iOS 12) | 1 |
| Unsafe Pointer Usage | 566 |
| Manual Reference Counting (Unmanaged<T>) | 213 |
